vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but requires significant revision based on visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is present. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are indistinguishable or absent as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor. A new, unidentified sprout is present in the foreground pot.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Mexican Mint)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Silver Guest)
inventory_reconciliation.New_Introduction: Unidentified monocot sprout in the foreground black pot.

plant_audit.P3: Significant chlorosis and necrosis on the primary leaf; the rabbit anchor remains stable.
plant_audit.Foreground_Pot: Contains a single, healthy-looking green sprout, likely a recent germination or propagation.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently moist, dark substrate across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White perlite/egg-shell fragments present on the surface of the foreground pot.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No fungal blooms detected; desk surface is clear of organic debris.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: Gradual yellowing and browning of the P3 leaf; the foreground sprout shows slow, steady vertical elongation.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_Current: The P3 leaf has reached a state of advanced senescence (browning). The foreground sprout remains turgid and upright.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently dominated by a single, struggling Pothos leaf (P3) and a new, unidentified sprout in the foreground. The String of Nickels (P1) remains in the background. The Pothos leaf is undergoing terminal senescence, likely due to root stress or light deficiency. The foreground sprout is the only sign of active, healthy growth in the system. The lack of P2 and P4 suggests either a failure to germinate or complete mortality of those specimens.
